name: 6 Day PPL Elite
description: V-taper + combat physique program. Push A/B, Pull A/B, Legs+Abs A/B repeated across 6 days. Based on home gym: dumbbells, pull-up/dip station, cable pulley, resistance bands, adjustable bench, punching bag. Beginner to advanced progression across 4 phases.

exercises.notes: Rotate palms from facing you to facing forward as you press. Hits all 3 delt heads. Primary V-taper builder. Increase weight when all 3 sets complete.
3 second negative on the way down. Elbows slightly bent. Most important exercise for shoulder width and V-taper. Never skip.
Flat bench. Full ROM. Elbows at 45 degrees to body, not flared. Touch chest at bottom. Chest width adds to upper body V shape.
Bench at 30-45 degrees. Upper chest focus. Upper chest development creates that full athletic look. Control the descent.
Use pulley system at shoulder height. Deep stretch at the bottom. Squeeze hard at the top. Weight is resistance, not the goal — feel the stretch.
Elbows pinned to sides throughout. Full extension at bottom. Triceps are 2/3 of arm size — build these for big arms.
Hold one heavy dumbbell with both hands overhead. Full stretch at bottom, full extension at top. Long head activation makes arms look bigger from behind.
Reps = seconds. 3 rounds of 1 minute. Hands up, exhale on every punch, alternate jab and cross. Shoulder endurance finisher. Burn out the delts.

days.name: Day 2 — Pull A (Back, Biceps, Rear Delts)

exercises.notes: Use pull-up station. Wide grip. Full hang at bottom, chin over bar at top. Use resistance band for assistance if needed. V-TAPER KING — never skip this exercise.
Hinge at hips 45 degrees, back flat. Drive elbows back not flared out. Squeeze at the top 1 second. Mid-back thickness. Core braced throughout.
Wide grip. Full stretch at the top — let shoulder blades rise. Pull to upper chest. Think about pulling elbows down to your hips, not pulling the bar.
Knee and same-side hand on bench. Opposite leg on floor. Full ROM — full hang at bottom. Go heavy here. Unilateral back thickness and strength.
Bent over at 45 degrees. Slow controlled arc upward. Rear delts create 3D shoulder look. Essential for posture and combat stability.
Bent over, pull dumbbells to face level with elbows high and flared. External rotation at the top. Rotator cuff health, posture correction, punch power base.
Bench at 60 degrees. Arms hang behind body at bottom for full stretch. Long head of bicep. Creates that peak when flexing. No swinging.
Neutral grip — thumbs up. Hits brachialis muscle which pushes bicep up. Makes arms look thicker and bigger from all angles. Alternate arms.
Reps = seconds. 3 rounds of 1 minute. Rotate hips into every hook. Uppercut rises from legs not arm. Power starts from the floor, not the shoulder.

days.name: Day 3 — Legs + Abs A (Quads, Glutes, Core, Kicks)

exercises.notes: Hold one heavy dumbbell at chest. Full depth — hips below knees. Knees track over toes. Core braced. Heels flat. Best beginner squat pattern.
Hip hinge, not squat. Push hips back, slight knee bend. Feel hamstring stretch at bottom. This is the biggest glute builder you have. Slow and controlled descent.
Step backward, not forward. Less knee stress. Front knee tracks toe. Back knee touches floor lightly. Dumbbells in hands. 12 reps each leg.
Band above knees throughout. Activates glute medius — the muscle that gives hip width. Also trains the hip stability needed for powerful kicks. Push knees out against band.
Stand on edge of step if available. Full ROM — heel below step at bottom, full rise at top. 3 second negative. Calves need high reps and volume to grow.
Lift knee to chest first, then extend leg forward. Hold 1 second extended. Slow and deliberate. Trains hip flexors and kick mechanics. 10 reps each leg. Wall for balance.
Hold wall or bag for balance. Lift leg laterally as high as hip flexibility allows. Hold 1 second. Hip abductor strength for side kick height. 10 reps each leg.
From knees. Roll out slowly, pause at full extension, roll back. Never let lower back sag. Best core exercise you own. Add 1 rep per week progressively.
Pull-up bar. Dead hang start. Pull knees to chest, lower SLOWLY. No swinging. Lower abs are the hardest to develop — these are the key exercise for them.
Reps = seconds. Front plank 30 sec, then side plank 30 sec each side = 1 set. Oblique definition comes from side planks. Hips never sag. Body perfectly straight.

days.name: Day 4 — Push B (Shoulder Focus, Chest, Triceps)

exercises.notes: Standing or seated. Elbows at 90 degrees at bottom. Full extension at top. Core braced to protect lower back. Primary mass builder for shoulders.
Alternate arms. Raise to shoulder height only — not higher. Slow and controlled. Anterior delt. No momentum or body swing. Light weight, strict form.
Do 12 reps at working weight, immediately drop to lighter weight and do 8 more without rest. That is 1 set. Lateral raises respond extremely well to drop sets — maximum shoulder pump.
Feet on bench, hands on floor. Shifts load to upper chest. Full ROM. Elbows at 45 degrees. This is your upper chest bodyweight press. Add reps every session.
Decline angle on bench. Lower chest definition. Hook feet under bench pad. Full ROM. Lower chest gives the chest a complete look from all angles.
Face away from anchor. Hands behind head. Extend fully. Long head of tricep stretch — this adds width and thickness to arms viewed from behind.
Hands form a diamond under chest. Elbows stay close to body. Medial and lateral tricep head. Bodyweight finisher. Go to failure in Phase 3+.
Reps = seconds. 3 rounds of 90 seconds. Jab-cross-hook-cross, then move head left or right. This is reflex and combination training. Stay light on feet between combos.

days.name: Day 5 — Pull B (Back Width, Thickness, Rear Chain)

exercises.notes: Shoulder-width or close grip. Different lat recruitment than wide grip. Full hang at bottom. Squeeze lats hard at top. Vary grip from Pull A for full lat development.
Use pulley system at floor level. Neutral grip attachment. Sit tall, retract scapula, pull to belly button. Mid-back thickness — the muscle that makes your back look 3D.
Heavy dumbbells. Straight up — do not roll shoulders. Hold at top 1 second. Trapezius development adds to athletic thick-necked warrior look.
Hold band in front, palms up (supinated). Pull apart to sides at shoulder height. Rear delts and external rotators. Arm health, posture, punching power base. Never skip.
Hinge 45 degrees. Arc dumbbells up and out. Pinch shoulder blades at top. Rear delts are often underdeveloped — these are what make shoulders look round and full from behind.
Seated. Elbow on inner thigh. Curl slowly. Maximum mind-muscle connection. This is peak isolation for bicep shape. 12 reps each arm. No body movement.
Both arms simultaneously or alternating. Neutral grip. Brachialis — the muscle that sits under the bicep and makes arms look thicker. Heavy and strict.
5 maximum-power punches each hand per set. Full wind-up. Rotate from the hip. Exhale and tighten core on impact. This trains POWER not cardio. Quality over speed here.

days.name: Day 6 — Legs + Abs B (Posterior Chain, Calves, Combat Core)

exercises.notes: Legs straighter than RDL — more hamstring focus. Hinge at hips. Bar travels close to shins. Full stretch at bottom. Slower tempo than RDL. Feel every inch of the hamstring.
Upper back on bench. Dumbbell on hips. Drive hips up, squeeze glutes hard at top 2 seconds. Lower slowly. Best glute max exercise. Powerful hips = powerful kicks.
Rear foot on bench. Front foot forward. Lower until rear knee nearly touches floor. Dumbbells in hands. Hardest leg exercise in this program. Do it anyway. 8 reps each leg.
On all fours. Band around ankle. Kick leg back and up. Squeeze glute at top. Glute medius and maximus isolation. Also trains the hip extension you need for front kicks.
4 second negative on every rep. Full stretch at bottom. Calves have slow-twitch fiber dominance — they respond to volume and time under tension, not just weight.
Squat down, explode up, land softly. Bend knees on landing to absorb impact. Explosive leg power directly transfers to kick power. Essential combat conditioning.
Phase 1-2: from knees. Phase 3: attempt from toes. Phase 4: single-arm or feet elevated. Add 1 rep every session. This is your core strength benchmark.
Hook feet on bench in decline. Sit up and rotate elbow to opposite knee. Obliques and upper abs. Add a dumbbell to chest when bodyweight becomes easy. Obliques create waist definition.
Pull-up bar. Raise straight legs to 90 degrees or higher. Lower SLOWLY — 3 seconds down. No swinging. Progression from knee raises. Lower abs and hip flexors. Critical for kick height.
